Static Docker Analyser

Report generated : 2026-05-02 23:37:34
Path scanned : ./DockerFiles/
Checks loaded : Excessive Linux Capabilities, Docker Socket Exposure, Unsafe Host Volume Mount, Host Network Mode, No Resource Limits, Privileged Container, Running as Root
WARNING [AppFlowy.yml]
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : LOW
Service : app
Value : /tmp/.X11-unix:/tmp/.X11-unix
Detail : Absolute host path '/tmp/.X11-unix' is bind-mounted into the container. The risk depends on the contents of this directory and cannot be confirmed by static analysis alone.
Fix : Review whether this bind mount is necessary and check the directory does not contain secrets or credentials. Prefer named Docker volumes where possible.
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : HIGH
Service : app
Value : /dev/dri:/dev/dri
Detail : Sensitive host path '/dev/dri' is mounted into the container, exposing critical system resources.
Fix : Remove the host path mount. Use named Docker volumes for persistent data and avoid mounting system directories into containers.
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : LOW
Service : app
Value : /var/run/dbus/system_bus_socket:/var/run/dbus/system_bus_socket
Detail : Absolute host path '/var/run/dbus/system_bus_socket' is bind-mounted into the container. The risk depends on the contents of this directory and cannot be confirmed by static analysis alone.
Fix : Review whether this bind mount is necessary and check the directory does not contain secrets or credentials. Prefer named Docker volumes where possible.
Check : Host Network Mode
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : app
Value : host
Detail : Removes Docker network isolation, container shares host network stack directly.
Fix : Use bridge networking and expose only required ports with the 'ports' directive.
Check : No Resource Limits
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : app
Value : no mem_limit or cpus set
Detail : No memory or CPU limits are set. This is a best practice violation that may increase risk if the container misbehaves or is compromised, potentially impacting other services on the host.
Fix : Set mem_limit and cpus at the service level, or define limits under deploy.resources.limits.
Check : Running as Root
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : app
Value : no user set
Detail : Informational: no user directive found in the Compose file. The container may run as root depending on the base image. If combined with other misconfigurations such as privileged mode or unsafe volume mounts this can become a medium risk issue.
Fix : Set user: to a non-root user or non-zero UID to explicitly prevent root execution regardless of the base image.

Check : Running as Root
Severity : HIGH
Service : web
Value : user: root
Detail : The container is explicitly configured to run as root. If compromised, an attacker has immediate root access within the container.
Fix : Create a non-root user in your Dockerfile and set user: to that user or a non-zero UID.

WARNING [actualbudget.yml]
Check : No Resource Limits
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : actual_server
Value : no mem_limit or cpus set
Detail : No memory or CPU limits are set. This is a best practice violation that may increase risk if the container misbehaves or is compromised, potentially impacting other services on the host.
Fix : Set mem_limit and cpus at the service level, or define limits under deploy.resources.limits.
Check : Running as Root
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : actual_server
Value : no user set
Detail : Informational: no user directive found in the Compose file. The container may run as root depending on the base image. If combined with other misconfigurations such as privileged mode or unsafe volume mounts this can become a medium risk issue.
Fix : Set user: to a non-root user or non-zero UID to explicitly prevent root execution regardless of the base image.
OK       [all-in-one.yaml]: no issues found
WARNING [appwrite.yml]
Check : Docker Socket Exposure
Severity : HIGH
Service : traefik
Value : /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
Detail : Mounting the Docker socket gives the container full control over the Docker daemon, enabling host takeover.
Fix : Remove the Docker socket mount. If container management access is required, restrict access using Unix socket permissions or protect the API with TLS.
Check : Docker Socket Exposure
Severity : HIGH
Service : appwrite
Value : /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
Detail : Mounting the Docker socket gives the container full control over the Docker daemon, enabling host takeover.
Fix : Remove the Docker socket mount. If container management access is required, restrict access using Unix socket permissions or protect the API with TLS.
Check : Docker Socket Exposure
Severity : HIGH
Service : openruntimes-executor
Value : /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
Detail : Mounting the Docker socket gives the container full control over the Docker daemon, enabling host takeover.
Fix : Remove the Docker socket mount. If container management access is required, restrict access using Unix socket permissions or protect the API with TLS.
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : LOW
Service : openruntimes-executor
Value : /tmp:/tmp:rw
Detail : Absolute host path '/tmp' is bind-mounted into the container. The risk depends on the contents of this directory and cannot be confirmed by static analysis alone.
Fix : Review whether this bind mount is necessary and check the directory does not contain secrets or credentials. Prefer named Docker volumes where possible.

Detail : Informational: no user directive found in the Compose file. The container may run as root depending on the base image. If combined with other misconfigurations such as privileged mode or unsafe volume mounts this can become a medium risk issue.
Fix : Set user: to a non-root user or non-zero UID to explicitly prevent root execution regardless of the base image.
WARNING [authentik.yml]
Check : Docker Socket Exposure
Severity : HIGH
Service : worker
Value : /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
Detail : Mounting the Docker socket gives the container full control over the Docker daemon, enabling host takeover.
Fix : Remove the Docker socket mount. If container management access is required, restrict access using Unix socket permissions or protect the API with TLS.
Check : No Resource Limits
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : postgresql
Value : no mem_limit or cpus set
Detail : No memory or CPU limits are set. This is a best practice violation that may increase risk if the container misbehaves or is compromised, potentially impacting other services on the host.
Fix : Set mem_limit and cpus at the service level, or define limits under deploy.resources.limits.
Check : No Resource Limits
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : server
Value : no mem_limit or cpus set
Detail : No memory or CPU limits are set. This is a best practice violation that may increase risk if the container misbehaves or is compromised, potentially impacting other services on the host.
Fix : Set mem_limit and cpus at the service level, or define limits under deploy.resources.limits.
Check : No Resource Limits
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : worker
Value : no mem_limit or cpus set
Detail : No memory or CPU limits are set. This is a best practice violation that may increase risk if the container misbehaves or is compromised, potentially impacting other services on the host.
Fix : Set mem_limit and cpus at the service level, or define limits under deploy.resources.limits.
Check : Running as Root
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : postgresql
Value : no user set
Detail : Informational: no user directive found in the Compose file. The container may run as root depending on the base image. If combined with other misconfigurations such as privileged mode or unsafe volume mounts this can become a medium risk issue.
Fix : Set user: to a non-root user or non-zero UID to explicitly prevent root execution regardless of the base image.
Check : Running as Root
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : server
Value : no user set
Detail : Informational: no user directive found in the Compose file. The container may run as root depending on the base image. If combined with other misconfigurations such as privileged mode or unsafe volume mounts this can become a medium risk issue.
Fix : Set user: to a non-root user or non-zero UID to explicitly prevent root execution regardless of the base image.
Check : Running as Root
Severity : HIGH
Service : worker
Value : user: root
Detail : The container is explicitly configured to run as root. If compromised, an attacker has immediate root access within the container.
Fix : Create a non-root user in your Dockerfile and set user: to that user or a non-zero UID.
WARNING [azuraCast.yml]
Check : Excessive Linux Capabilities
Severity : HIGH
Service : web
Value : cap_add: SYS_PTRACE
Detail : Capability 'SYS_PTRACE' grants broad kernel-level access and significantly undermines container isolation.
Fix : Remove this capability. If specific functionality is required, identify the minimal capability needed and document the justification.

Value : no mem_limit or cpus set
Detail : No memory or CPU limits are set. This is a best practice violation that may increase risk if the container misbehaves or is compromised, potentially impacting other services on the host.
Fix : Set mem_limit and cpus at the service level, or define limits under deploy.resources.limits.
Check : Running as Root
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : coolify
Value : no user set
Detail : Informational: no user directive found in the Compose file. The container may run as root depending on the base image. If combined with other misconfigurations such as privileged mode or unsafe volume mounts this can become a medium risk issue.
Fix : Set user: to a non-root user or non-zero UID to explicitly prevent root execution regardless of the base image.

Check : Privileged Container
Severity : HIGH
Service : opengauss
Value : privileged: true
Detail : Grants the container full access to the host kernel, making container escape trivial.
Fix : Remove privileged mode and grant only the specific Linux capabilities the container needs.

Severity : MEDIUM
Service : init_permissions
Value : no user set
Detail : Informational: no user directive found in the Compose file. The container may run as root depending on the base image. If combined with other misconfigurations such as privileged mode or unsafe volume mounts this can become a medium risk issue.
Fix : Set user: to a non-root user or non-zero UID to explicitly prevent root execution regardless of the base image.

Severity : LOW
Service : calibre-web
Value : /path/to/calibre-web/data:/config
Detail : Absolute host path '/path/to/calibre-web/data' is bind-mounted into the container. The risk depends on the contents of this directory and cannot be confirmed by static analysis alone.
Fix : Review whether this bind mount is necessary and check the directory does not contain secrets or credentials. Prefer named Docker volumes where possible.
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : LOW
Service : calibre-web
Value : /path/to/calibre/library:/books
Detail : Absolute host path '/path/to/calibre/library' is bind-mounted into the container. The risk depends on the contents of this directory and cannot be confirmed by static analysis alone.
Fix : Review whether this bind mount is necessary and check the directory does not contain secrets or credentials. Prefer named Docker volumes where possible.
Check : No Resource Limits
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : calibre-web
Value : no mem_limit or cpus set
Detail : No memory or CPU limits are set. This is a best practice violation that may increase risk if the container misbehaves or is compromised, potentially impacting other services on the host.
Fix : Set mem_limit and cpus at the service level, or define limits under deploy.resources.limits.
Check : Running as Root
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : calibre-web
Value : no user set
Detail : Informational: no user directive found in the Compose file. The container may run as root depending on the base image. If combined with other misconfigurations such as privileged mode or unsafe volume mounts this can become a medium risk issue.
Fix : Set user: to a non-root user or non-zero UID to explicitly prevent root execution regardless of the base image.

WARNING [docker-homebridge.yml]
Check : Host Network Mode
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : homebridge
Value : host
Detail : Removes Docker network isolation, container shares host network stack directly.
Fix : Use bridge networking and expose only required ports with the 'ports' directive.

Value : no user set
Detail : Informational: no user directive found in the Compose file. The container may run as root depending on the base image. If combined with other misconfigurations such as privileged mode or unsafe volume mounts this can become a medium risk issue.
Fix : Set user: to a non-root user or non-zero UID to explicitly prevent root execution regardless of the base image.
WARNING [flagsmith.yml]
Check : No Resource Limits
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : postgres
Value : no mem_limit or cpus set
Detail : No memory or CPU limits are set. This is a best practice violation that may increase risk if the container misbehaves or is compromised, potentially impacting other services on the host.
Fix : Set mem_limit and cpus at the service level, or define limits under deploy.resources.limits.

WARNING [grocy.yml]
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : LOW
Service : grocy
Value : /path/to/grocy/config:/config
Detail : Absolute host path '/path/to/grocy/config' is bind-mounted into the container. The risk depends on the contents of this directory and cannot be confirmed by static analysis alone.
Fix : Review whether this bind mount is necessary and check the directory does not contain secrets or credentials. Prefer named Docker volumes where possible.

WARNING [home-assistant-config.yml]
Check : Excessive Linux Capabilities
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : homeassistant
Value : cap_add: NET_ADMIN
Detail : Capability 'NET_ADMIN' expands the container attack surface and may be exploited depending on the deployment context.
Fix : Review whether this capability is strictly necessary. If so, ensure the container is otherwise hardened and the justification is documented.
Check : Excessive Linux Capabilities
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : homeassistant
Value : cap_add: NET_RAW
Detail : Capability 'NET_RAW' expands the container attack surface and may be exploited depending on the deployment context.
Fix : Review whether this capability is strictly necessary. If so, ensure the container is otherwise hardened and the justification is documented.
Check : Excessive Linux Capabilities
Severity : HIGH
Service : music-assistant
Value : cap_add: SYS_ADMIN
Detail : Capability 'SYS_ADMIN' grants broad kernel-level access and significantly undermines container isolation.
Fix : Remove this capability. If specific functionality is required, identify the minimal capability needed and document the justification.
Check : Excessive Linux Capabilities
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : music-assistant
Value : cap_add: DAC_READ_SEARCH
Detail : Capability 'DAC_READ_SEARCH' expands the container attack surface and may be exploited depending on the deployment context.
Fix : Review whether this capability is strictly necessary. If so, ensure the container is otherwise hardened and the justification is documented.
Check : Docker Socket Exposure
Severity : HIGH
Service : portainer
Value : /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
Detail : Mounting the Docker socket gives the container full control over the Docker daemon, enabling host takeover.
Fix : Remove the Docker socket mount. If container management access is required, restrict access using Unix socket permissions or protect the API with TLS.
Check : Docker Socket Exposure
Severity : HIGH
Service : vscode
Value : /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
Detail : Mounting the Docker socket gives the container full control over the Docker daemon, enabling host takeover.
Fix : Remove the Docker socket mount. If container management access is required, restrict access using Unix socket permissions or protect the API with TLS.
Check : Docker Socket Exposure
Severity : HIGH
Service : watchtower
Value : /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
Detail : Mounting the Docker socket gives the container full control over the Docker daemon, enabling host takeover.
Fix : Remove the Docker socket mount. If container management access is required, restrict access using Unix socket permissions or protect the API with TLS.
Check : Docker Socket Exposure
Severity : HIGH
Service : traefik
Value : /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
Detail : Mounting the Docker socket gives the container full control over the Docker daemon, enabling host takeover.
Fix : Remove the Docker socket mount. If container management access is required, restrict access using Unix socket permissions or protect the API with TLS.
Check : Docker Socket Exposure
Severity : HIGH
Service : sablier
Value : /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
Detail : Mounting the Docker socket gives the container full control over the Docker daemon, enabling host takeover.
Fix : Remove the Docker socket mount. If container management access is required, restrict access using Unix socket permissions or protect the API with TLS.
Check : Docker Socket Exposure
Severity : HIGH
Service : tfc-agent
Value : /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
Detail : Mounting the Docker socket gives the container full control over the Docker daemon, enabling host takeover.
Fix : Remove the Docker socket mount. If container management access is required, restrict access using Unix socket permissions or protect the API with TLS.
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : LOW
Service : homeassistant
Value : /opt/homeassistant:/config
Detail : Absolute host path '/opt/homeassistant' is bind-mounted into the container. The risk depends on the contents of this directory and cannot be confirmed by static analysis alone.
Fix : Review whether this bind mount is necessary and check the directory does not contain secrets or credentials. Prefer named Docker volumes where possible.
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : HIGH
Service : homeassistant
Value : /home/aneisch/symlinks_for_rsync/hass-docker-ssh:/root/.ssh:ro
Detail : Sensitive host path '/home/aneisch/symlinks_for_rsync/hass-docker-ssh' is mounted into the container, exposing critical system resources.
Fix : Remove the host path mount. Use named Docker volumes for persistent data and avoid mounting system directories into containers.
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : LOW
Service : homeassistant
Value : /tmp/radar.gif:/tmp/radar.gif
Detail : Absolute host path '/tmp/radar.gif' is bind-mounted into the container. The risk depends on the contents of this directory and cannot be confirmed by static analysis alone.
Fix : Review whether this bind mount is necessary and check the directory does not contain secrets or credentials. Prefer named Docker volumes where possible.
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : HIGH
Service : homeassistant
Value : /sys/class/hwmon/hwmon1/temp1_input:/sys/class/thermal/thermal_zone0/temp:ro
Detail : Sensitive host path '/sys/class/hwmon/hwmon1/temp1_input' is mounted into the container, exposing critical system resources.
Fix : Remove the host path mount. Use named Docker volumes for persistent data and avoid mounting system directories into containers.

Check : Host Network Mode
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : homeassistant
Value : host
Detail : Removes Docker network isolation, container shares host network stack directly.
Fix : Use bridge networking and expose only required ports with the 'ports' directive.

Check : No Resource Limits
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : homeassistant
Value : no mem_limit or cpus set
Detail : No memory or CPU limits are set. This is a best practice violation that may increase risk if the container misbehaves or is compromised, potentially impacting other services on the host.
Fix : Set mem_limit and cpus at the service level, or define limits under deploy.resources.limits.
Check : No Resource Limits

Detail : No memory or CPU limits are set. This is a best practice violation that may increase risk if the container misbehaves or is compromised, potentially impacting other services on the host.
Fix : Set mem_limit and cpus at the service level, or define limits under deploy.resources.limits.
Check : No Resource Limits
Severity : MEDIUM

Fix : Set mem_limit and cpus at the service level, or define limits under deploy.resources.limits.

Check : Privileged Container
Severity : HIGH
Service : tailscale
Value : privileged: true
Detail : Grants the container full access to the host kernel, making container escape trivial.
Fix : Remove privileged mode and grant only the specific Linux capabilities the container needs.
Check : Privileged Container
Severity : HIGH
Service : frigate
Value : privileged: true
Detail : Grants the container full access to the host kernel, making container escape trivial.
Fix : Remove privileged mode and grant only the specific Linux capabilities the container needs.
Check : Privileged Container
Severity : HIGH
Service : auto-southwest
Value : privileged: true
Detail : Grants the container full access to the host kernel, making container escape trivial.
Fix : Remove privileged mode and grant only the specific Linux capabilities the container needs.
Check : Running as Root
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : homeassistant
Value : no user set
Detail : Informational: no user directive found in the Compose file. The container may run as root depending on the base image. If combined with other misconfigurations such as privileged mode or unsafe volume mounts this can become a medium risk issue.
Fix : Set user: to a non-root user or non-zero UID to explicitly prevent root execution regardless of the base image.
Check : Running as Root
Severity : MEDIUM

Check : Running as Root
Severity : HIGH
Service : tfc-agent
Value : user: root
Detail : The container is explicitly configured to run as root. If compromised, an attacker has immediate root access within the container.
Fix : Create a non-root user in your Dockerfile and set user: to that user or a non-zero UID.
WARNING [home-assistant.io.yml]
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : LOW
Service : homeassistant
Value : /PATH_TO_YOUR_CONFIG:/config
Detail : Absolute host path '/PATH_TO_YOUR_CONFIG' is bind-mounted into the container. The risk depends on the contents of this directory and cannot be confirmed by static analysis alone.
Fix : Review whether this bind mount is necessary and check the directory does not contain secrets or credentials. Prefer named Docker volumes where possible.
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : LOW
Service : homeassistant
Value : /run/dbus:/run/dbus:ro
Detail : Absolute host path '/run/dbus' is bind-mounted into the container. The risk depends on the contents of this directory and cannot be confirmed by static analysis alone.
Fix : Review whether this bind mount is necessary and check the directory does not contain secrets or credentials. Prefer named Docker volumes where possible.
Check : Host Network Mode
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : homeassistant
Value : host
Detail : Removes Docker network isolation, container shares host network stack directly.
Fix : Use bridge networking and expose only required ports with the 'ports' directive.
Check : No Resource Limits
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : homeassistant
Value : no mem_limit or cpus set
Detail : No memory or CPU limits are set. This is a best practice violation that may increase risk if the container misbehaves or is compromised, potentially impacting other services on the host.
Fix : Set mem_limit and cpus at the service level, or define limits under deploy.resources.limits.
Check : Privileged Container
Severity : HIGH
Service : homeassistant
Value : privileged: true
Detail : Grants the container full access to the host kernel, making container escape trivial.
Fix : Remove privileged mode and grant only the specific Linux capabilities the container needs.
Check : Running as Root
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : homeassistant
Value : no user set
Detail : Informational: no user directive found in the Compose file. The container may run as root depending on the base image. If combined with other misconfigurations such as privileged mode or unsafe volume mounts this can become a medium risk issue.
Fix : Set user: to a non-root user or non-zero UID to explicitly prevent root execution regardless of the base image.

Fix : Set user: to a non-root user or non-zero UID to explicitly prevent root execution regardless of the base image.
WARNING [immich-app.yml]
Check : No Resource Limits
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : immich-server
Value : no mem_limit or cpus set
Detail : No memory or CPU limits are set. This is a best practice violation that may increase risk if the container misbehaves or is compromised, potentially impacting other services on the host.
Fix : Set mem_limit and cpus at the service level, or define limits under deploy.resources.limits.

WARNING [linuxserver.io.yml]
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : LOW
Service : code-server
Value : /path/to/code-server/config:/config
Detail : Absolute host path '/path/to/code-server/config' is bind-mounted into the container. The risk depends on the contents of this directory and cannot be confirmed by static analysis alone.
Fix : Review whether this bind mount is necessary and check the directory does not contain secrets or credentials. Prefer named Docker volumes where possible.

WARNING [loki.yml]
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : LOW
Service : fluentbit
Value : /var/log/syslog:/var/log/syslog:ro
Detail : Absolute host path '/var/log/syslog' is bind-mounted into the container. The risk depends on the contents of this directory and cannot be confirmed by static analysis alone.
Fix : Review whether this bind mount is necessary and check the directory does not contain secrets or credentials. Prefer named Docker volumes where possible.

Check : Running as Root
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : fluentd
Value : no user set
Detail : Informational: no user directive found in the Compose file. The container may run as root depending on the base image. If combined with other misconfigurations such as privileged mode or unsafe volume mounts this can become a medium risk issue.
Fix : Set user: to a non-root user or non-zero UID to explicitly prevent root execution regardless of the base image.
Check : Running as Root
Severity : HIGH
Service : fluentbit
Value : user: root
Detail : The container is explicitly configured to run as root. If compromised, an attacker has immediate root access within the container.
Fix : Create a non-root user in your Dockerfile and set user: to that user or a non-zero UID.

Detail : Informational: no user directive found in the Compose file. The container may run as root depending on the base image. If combined with other misconfigurations such as privileged mode or unsafe volume mounts this can become a medium risk issue.
Fix : Set user: to a non-root user or non-zero UID to explicitly prevent root execution regardless of the base image.

WARNING [nocodb.yml]
Check : No Resource Limits
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : nocodb
Value : no mem_limit or cpus set
Detail : No memory or CPU limits are set. This is a best practice violation that may increase risk if the container misbehaves or is compromised, potentially impacting other services on the host.
Fix : Set mem_limit and cpus at the service level, or define limits under deploy.resources.limits.

WARNING [openproject.yml]
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : HIGH
Service : chrome
Value : /dev/shm:/dev/shm
Detail : Sensitive host path '/dev/shm' is mounted into the container, exposing critical system resources.
Fix : Remove the host path mount. Use named Docker volumes for persistent data and avoid mounting system directories into containers.
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : HIGH
Service : firefox
Value : /dev/shm:/dev/shm
Detail : Sensitive host path '/dev/shm' is mounted into the container, exposing critical system resources.
Fix : Remove the host path mount. Use named Docker volumes for persistent data and avoid mounting system directories into containers.

WARNING [photoprism.yml]
Check : No Resource Limits
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : photoprism
Value : no mem_limit or cpus set
Detail : No memory or CPU limits are set. This is a best practice violation that may increase risk if the container misbehaves or is compromised, potentially impacting other services on the host.
Fix : Set mem_limit and cpus at the service level, or define limits under deploy.resources.limits.
Check : No Resource Limits
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : mariadb
Value : no mem_limit or cpus set
Detail : No memory or CPU limits are set. This is a best practice violation that may increase risk if the container misbehaves or is compromised, potentially impacting other services on the host.
Fix : Set mem_limit and cpus at the service level, or define limits under deploy.resources.limits.
Check : Privileged Container
Severity : HIGH
Service : photoprism
Value : privileged: true
Detail : Grants the container full access to the host kernel, making container escape trivial.
Fix : Remove privileged mode and grant only the specific Linux capabilities the container needs.
Check : Running as Root
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : photoprism
Value : no user set

WARNING [portainer.yml]
Check : Docker Socket Exposure
Severity : HIGH
Service : portainer
Value : /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
Detail : Mounting the Docker socket gives the container full control over the Docker daemon, enabling host takeover.
Fix : Remove the Docker socket mount. If container management access is required, restrict access using Unix socket permissions or protect the API with TLS.
Check : No Resource Limits
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : portainer
Value : no mem_limit or cpus set
Detail : No memory or CPU limits are set. This is a best practice violation that may increase risk if the container misbehaves or is compromised, potentially impacting other services on the host.
Fix : Set mem_limit and cpus at the service level, or define limits under deploy.resources.limits.
Check : Running as Root
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : portainer
Value : no user set
Detail : Informational: no user directive found in the Compose file. The container may run as root depending on the base image. If combined with other misconfigurations such as privileged mode or unsafe volume mounts this can become a medium risk issue.
Fix : Set user: to a non-root user or non-zero UID to explicitly prevent root execution regardless of the base image.
WARNING [prometheus.yml]
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : HIGH
Service : node-exporter
Value : /proc:/host/proc:ro
Detail : Sensitive host path '/proc' is mounted into the container, exposing critical system resources.
Fix : Remove the host path mount. Use named Docker volumes for persistent data and avoid mounting system directories into containers.
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : HIGH
Service : node-exporter
Value : /sys:/host/sys:ro
Detail : Sensitive host path '/sys' is mounted into the container, exposing critical system resources.
Fix : Remove the host path mount. Use named Docker volumes for persistent data and avoid mounting system directories into containers.
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : HIGH
Service : node-exporter
Value : /:/rootfs:ro
Detail : Sensitive host path '/' is mounted into the container, exposing critical system resources.
Fix : Remove the host path mount. Use named Docker volumes for persistent data and avoid mounting system directories into containers.
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : HIGH
Service : node-exporter
Value : /:/host:ro,rslave
Detail : Sensitive host path '/' is mounted into the container, exposing critical system resources.
Fix : Remove the host path mount. Use named Docker volumes for persistent data and avoid mounting system directories into containers.
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : HIGH
Service : cadvisor
Value : /:/rootfs:ro
Detail : Sensitive host path '/' is mounted into the container, exposing critical system resources.
Fix : Remove the host path mount. Use named Docker volumes for persistent data and avoid mounting system directories into containers.
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : LOW
Service : cadvisor
Value : /var/run:/var/run:rw
Detail : Absolute host path '/var/run' is bind-mounted into the container. The risk depends on the contents of this directory and cannot be confirmed by static analysis alone.
Fix : Review whether this bind mount is necessary and check the directory does not contain secrets or credentials. Prefer named Docker volumes where possible.
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : HIGH
Service : cadvisor
Value : /sys:/sys:ro
Detail : Sensitive host path '/sys' is mounted into the container, exposing critical system resources.
Fix : Remove the host path mount. Use named Docker volumes for persistent data and avoid mounting system directories into containers.
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : LOW
Service : cadvisor
Value : /var/lib/docker/:/var/lib/docker:ro
Detail : Absolute host path '/var/lib/docker/' is bind-mounted into the container. The risk depends on the contents of this directory and cannot be confirmed by static analysis alone.
Fix : Review whether this bind mount is necessary and check the directory does not contain secrets or credentials. Prefer named Docker volumes where possible.
Check : No Resource Limits
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : prometheus
Value : no mem_limit or cpus set
Detail : No memory or CPU limits are set. This is a best practice violation that may increase risk if the container misbehaves or is compromised, potentially impacting other services on the host.
Fix : Set mem_limit and cpus at the service level, or define limits under deploy.resources.limits.

Fix : Set user: to a non-root user or non-zero UID to explicitly prevent root execution regardless of the base image.

WARNING [supabase.yml]
Check : Docker Socket Exposure
Severity : HIGH
Service : vector
Value : ${DOCKER_SOCKET_LOCATION}:/var/run/docker.sock:ro,z
Detail : Mounting the Docker socket gives the container full control over the Docker daemon, enabling host takeover.
Fix : Remove the Docker socket mount. If container management access is required, restrict access using Unix socket permissions or protect the API with TLS.

Fix : Set user: to a non-root user or non-zero UID to explicitly prevent root execution regardless of the base image.
Check : Running as Root
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : proxy
Value : no user set
Detail : Informational: no user directive found in the Compose file. The container may run as root depending on the base image. If combined with other misconfigurations such as privileged mode or unsafe volume mounts this can become a medium risk issue.
Fix : Set user: to a non-root user or non-zero UID to explicitly prevent root execution regardless of the base image.
WARNING [traefik.yml]
Check : Docker Socket Exposure
Severity : HIGH
Service : traefik
Value : /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
Detail : Mounting the Docker socket gives the container full control over the Docker daemon, enabling host takeover.
Fix : Remove the Docker socket mount. If container management access is required, restrict access using Unix socket permissions or protect the API with TLS.
Check : No Resource Limits
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : traefik
Value : no mem_limit or cpus set
Detail : No memory or CPU limits are set. This is a best practice violation that may increase risk if the container misbehaves or is compromised, potentially impacting other services on the host.
Fix : Set mem_limit and cpus at the service level, or define limits under deploy.resources.limits.

Fix : Set mem_limit and cpus at the service level, or define limits under deploy.resources.limits.

WARNING [wallabag.yml]
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : LOW
Service : wallabag
Value : /opt/wallabag/images:/var/www/wallabag/web/assets/images
Detail : Absolute host path '/opt/wallabag/images' is bind-mounted into the container. The risk depends on the contents of this directory and cannot be confirmed by static analysis alone.
Fix : Review whether this bind mount is necessary and check the directory does not contain secrets or credentials. Prefer named Docker volumes where possible.
Check : Unsafe Host Volume Mount
Severity : LOW
Service : db
Value : /opt/wallabag/data:/var/lib/mysql
Detail : Absolute host path '/opt/wallabag/data' is bind-mounted into the container. The risk depends on the contents of this directory and cannot be confirmed by static analysis alone.
Fix : Review whether this bind mount is necessary and check the directory does not contain secrets or credentials. Prefer named Docker volumes where possible.

Detail : No memory or CPU limits are set. This is a best practice violation that may increase risk if the container misbehaves or is compromised, potentially impacting other services on the host.
Fix : Set mem_limit and cpus at the service level, or define limits under deploy.resources.limits.
Check : No Resource Limits
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : caddy
Value : no mem_limit or cpus set
Detail : No memory or CPU limits are set. This is a best practice violation that may increase risk if the container misbehaves or is compromised, potentially impacting other services on the host.
Fix : Set mem_limit and cpus at the service level, or define limits under deploy.resources.limits.
Check : Privileged Container
Severity : HIGH
Service : dind
Value : privileged: true
Detail : Grants the container full access to the host kernel, making container escape trivial.
Fix : Remove privileged mode and grant only the specific Linux capabilities the container needs.
Check : Privileged Container
Severity : HIGH
Service : windmill_worker
Value : privileged: true
Detail : Grants the container full access to the host kernel, making container escape trivial.
Fix : Remove privileged mode and grant only the specific Linux capabilities the container needs.
Check : Running as Root
Severity : MEDIUM
Service : db
Value : no user set
Detail : Informational: no user directive found in the Compose file. The container may run as root depending on the base image. If combined with other misconfigurations such as privileged mode or unsafe volume mounts this can become a medium risk issue.
Fix : Set user: to a non-root user or non-zero UID to explicitly prevent root execution regardless of the base image.

Severity : MEDIUM

Check : Running as Root
Severity : HIGH
Service : zammad-backup
Value : user: 0:0
Detail : The container is explicitly configured to run as root. If compromised, an attacker has immediate root access within the container.
Fix : Create a non-root user in your Dockerfile and set user: to that user or a non-zero UID.
